reasoning: The USD/MXN spot rate on Feb 28, 2025 was 20.53, placing it in the upper half of its recent 20.10–20.73 trading range. Short-term momentum has shown signs of exhaustion at these higher levels, and the FRED statistical tool predicts a ~0.32 probability of the rate being higher than 20.53 by March 9, owing to a modest mean-reverting downward trend. Consequently, the short-term probability of an increase relative to the March 2 baseline is below 0.5 (estimated at 0.33). Over longer horizons, foreign exchange markets increasingly behave as random walks (martingales), making the direction of change notoriously difficult to forecast. Thus, the probability of an increase decays toward 0.5 over time. This sequence perfectly adheres to the structural constraint that uncertainty must increase (meaning absolute distance from 0.5 must decrease) with the length of the forecast horizon.
